title: optimoptions
summary: Create or update solver-specific optimization options for fminbnd, fzero, fsolve, and lsqcurvefit.

gpu_support.notes: Options are host metadata. gpuArray option values are gathered before validation.

description: `optimoptions(solver)` creates a solver-specific options struct with RunMat defaults. `optimoptions(solver, name, value, ...)` applies validated name-value overrides, and `optimoptions(oldOptions, name, value, ...)` copies an existing options struct before applying later values.
behaviors: Option names are case-insensitive and stored with canonical field names: `TolX`, `TolFun`, `MaxIter`, `MaxFunEvals`, `Display`, and `Algorithm`.
Modern aliases are accepted: `StepTolerance` maps to `TolX`, and `FunctionTolerance` and `OptimalityTolerance` map to `TolFun`.
`TolX` and `TolFun` must be finite positive numeric scalars. `MaxIter` and `MaxFunEvals` must be finite positive integer scalars.
`Display` accepts `off`, `none`, `iter`, and `final` for `fzero`, `fsolve`, and `lsqcurvefit`; `fminbnd` also accepts `notify` and defaults to it.
`lsqcurvefit` accepts `Algorithm` values `levenberg-marquardt` and `trust-region-reflective`; both currently configure RunMat's projected Levenberg-Marquardt solver.
Unknown options are rejected so solver configuration mistakes fail before the solver starts.
The returned struct can be passed directly to `fminbnd`, `fzero`, `fsolve`, and `lsqcurvefit`.

examples.input:
opts = optimoptions("fminbnd", "TolX", 1e-10, "Display", "off");
x = fminbnd(@cos, 0, pi, opts)

opts = optimoptions("lsqcurvefit", "StepTolerance", 1e-8, "Algorithm", "levenberg-marquardt");
p = lsqcurvefit(@(p,x) p(1).*x + p(2), [0; 0], [0 1 2], [1 3 5], [], [], opts)

faqs.question: How is this different from optimset?
faqs.answer: `optimoptions` starts from solver-specific defaults and validates option names and values immediately. `optimset` is a looser compatibility helper that preserves unknown fields.

faqs.question: Does optimoptions run on the GPU?
faqs.answer: No. Options structs are CPU metadata. If an option value is a gpuArray scalar, RunMat gathers it before validation.

faqs.question: Can I pass optimoptions output to existing solvers?
faqs.answer: Yes. `fminbnd`, `fzero`, `fsolve`, and `lsqcurvefit` consume the canonical option fields returned by `optimoptions`.

gpu_residency: Options structs are host metadata. RunMat gathers gpuArray option values before validation and returns a host struct.
gpu_behavior: `optimoptions` does not need a provider kernel because it constructs and validates metadata for host iterative solvers.
The builtin registers GPU and fusion specs as a host metadata sink so fusion planning does not try to move options construction onto the device.
